## 🔴 High Severity — Real Bugs

---

### BACKEND-H4 — Null-dereference crash on unknown SKU
- **Repo:** designflow-backend
- **File:** `services/autofill.service.js:79-83`
- **Confidence:** Certain
- **What it is:** `getPropertyById(itemData.udf_merchgroup06_fk_id)` runs *before* the null check `if (!itemData) return null`. When `getItemBySku` returns null (SKU not found), the code throws `TypeError: Cannot read properties of null`.
- **What it causes:** Any autofill request for an unknown SKU crashes. The autofill routes also have no auth guard, so this is publicly reachable.

---

### BACKEND-H5 — "Select vendor" writes to the wrong database table
- **Repo:** designflow-backend
- **File:** `models/lib.model.js:2266-2282`
- **Confidence:** Likely
- **What it is:** `Customer.selectedVendor` calls `sql.GridLayout.update(req.body, { where: { rfqItem_style_number } })`, but `GridLayout` has no `rfqItem_style_number` column. The update targets the wrong table entirely.
- **What it causes:** Choosing a vendor likely never actually saves. The operation either errors silently or updates 0 rows, and the selection is lost.

---

### BACKEND-H6 — `findRFQAddProductLayout` references an undefined variable
- **Repo:** designflow-backend
- **File:** `models/lib.model.js:641, 666`
- **Confidence:** Certain
- **What it is:** Inside a loop over `data`, two branches read/write `newData[i]`, but `newData` is never declared anywhere in the function.
- **What it causes:** `ReferenceError` the moment either branch is hit, breaking the Add-Product layout endpoint.

---

### DATA-SYNCING-H1 — Item-header, attachment, and merch-group-header syncs silently write nothing
- **Repo:** designflow-data-syncing
- **File:** `models/lib.model.js:61-114`
- **Confidence:** Certain
- **What it is:** `findOrCreate()` only has logic for `schemaName === "merchGroup"`. It is called with `'itemHeader'` (line 214), `'itemAttachment'` (line 229), and `'merchGroupHeaders'` (line 637). For those, the function does nothing and returns `[]`.
- **What it causes:** The `/getItemHeaderFromCL` and image-sync flows fetch everything from ColdLion and then silently discard it — not a single record is written to the database, and no error is reported.

---

### DATA-SYNCING-H2 — Image upload calls a route that doesn't exist
- **Repo:** designflow-data-syncing
- **File:** `models/lib.model.js:497-517`
- **Confidence:** Certain
- **What it is:** `uploadToGCS()` POSTs to `http://localhost:5001/uploadToGCS`, which is this service's own port, and no such route exists in `routes/`. The 404 is swallowed (returns `undefined`), then line 319 calls `publicUrl.replace(...)` on `undefined`.
- **What it causes:** A `TypeError` aborts the entire image sync on every run. No product images are ever uploaded to cloud storage.

---

### DATA-SYNCING-H3 — Vendor and customer update step crashes on an undefined variable
- **Repo:** designflow-data-syncing
- **File:** `models/lib.model.js:836-840, 867-871`
- **Confidence:** Certain
- **What it is:** Inside the `.then` of `getExternalVendor`/`getExternalCustomer`, the `where` condition references `data[i]`, but `i` is not in scope → `ReferenceError` on every run.
- **What it causes:** Existing vendor and customer rows are **never updated** — only brand-new records are inserted. The error is returned as an HTTP 200 "success" so it's been going unnoticed.

---

### DATA-SYNCING-H4 — Duplicate merch-group rows created when DB data is newer
- **Repo:** designflow-data-syncing
- **File:** `models/lib.model.js:161-170`
- **Confidence:** Certain
- **What it is:** `findOrCreateWithSSE` adds `modTime: { [Op.lte]: modTime }` to the `findOrCreate` where-clause. If the existing DB row has a *newer* `modTime`, the lookup misses and a duplicate row with older data is created. The `merchGroup` table has no unique constraint on the composite key.
- **What it causes:** Duplicate merch-group rows grow over time, corrupting merch-group data.

---

### DATA-SYNCING-H5 — Freeform/date fields are silently nulled on every sync
- **Repo:** designflow-data-syncing
- **File:** `helpers/utility.js:50-55 vs 127-133`
- **Confidence:** Certain
- **What it is:** An object literal sets `udf_freeform_01..04` and `udf_freeform_03` (brandAssuranceNo) from ColdLion data, then later duplicate keys in the same literal overwrite them with `null` (last key wins in JavaScript).
- **What it causes:** Every synced item permanently loses those freeform field values on every sync run.

---

### DATA-SYNCING-H6 — Division-code mapping is always hardcoded to 2
- **Repo:** designflow-data-syncing
- **File:** `helpers/utility.js:25-32`
- **Confidence:** Certain
- **What it is:** `if (item.divisionCode == 'divisionCode')` compares against the literal string `'divisionCode'`, which is never true. So the else-branch runs for every single item and sets `div_code_fk = 2`.
- **What it causes:** Every synced item gets division code 2 regardless of its actual division in ColdLion.

---

### DATA-SYNCING-H7 — Batch splitter drops up to ~749 items from image sync
- **Repo:** designflow-data-syncing
- **File:** `models/lib.model.js:223-230`
- **Confidence:** Certain
- **What it is:** `processSplitter = Math.round(allItems.length / 1500)` uses `round` instead of `ceil`. For example, 2,200 items → 1 batch of 1,500, and 700 items are never processed.
- **What it causes:** Roughly the last third of items in any large image-sync run are silently skipped.

---

### DATA-SYNCING-H8 — Retry loop can spin forever
- **Repo:** designflow-data-syncing
- **File:** `models/lib.model.js:446-481`
- **Confidence:** Certain
- **What it is:** `while (j < 5 || parseInt(head.headers['content-length']) == 200)` confuses HTTP status with content-length. Any file whose size is exactly 200 bytes triggers the `|| 200` branch and loops forever.
- **What it causes:** A 200-byte image file hangs the entire sync indefinitely.

---

### DATA-SYNCING-H9 — File upload starts before the image is written to disk
- **Repo:** designflow-data-syncing
- **File:** `models/lib.model.js:519-529`
- **Confidence:** Certain
- **What it is:** `storePictures` uses callback-style `fs.writeFile` and returns immediately (the `await` is meaningless on a non-Promise callback). `uploadToGCS` is then called with the file path before the write completes.
- **What it causes:** Uploads of truncated or empty files. Race condition on every image sync.

---

### DATA-SYNCING-H10 — Two tables hardcoded to wrong database schemas
- **Repo:** designflow-data-syncing
- **File:** `models/db/GridLayout.js:95`, `models/db/viewlayout.js:44`
- **Confidence:** Certain
- **What it is:** `schema: 'designflow_sandbox'` and `schema: 'rfq_backoffice13_prod'` are hardcoded strings overriding the environment `SCHEMA` variable.
- **What it causes:** These two tables are always created and read from sandbox/prod schemas regardless of environment. Production queries can bleed into sandbox data and vice versa.

---

### ITEM-MASTER-H3 — Infinite loop on item-number collision
- **Repo:** designflow-item-master
- **File:** `services/item_library.service.js:627-659`
- **Confidence:** Certain
- **What it is:** In `checkRFQStyleNumber` and `checkItemHeaderItemNumber`, if a matching record exists, `priceCodeIsChecked` never becomes `true` and the `while` loop re-runs the same `findOne` query indefinitely.
- **What it causes:** Any naming collision during item creation hangs the request permanently and hammers the database with repeated identical queries until the server process is killed.

---

### ITEM-MASTER-H4 — `updateProdOrderHeader` endpoint hangs every request
- **Repo:** designflow-item-master
- **File:** `services/item_library.service.js:419-421`, `services/art_piece.service.js:250-252`
- **Confidence:** Certain
- **What it is:** Both implementations reference an undefined variable `data`, throwing a `ReferenceError` inside an async callback with no catch. The callback is never invoked.

# dflow PLM — Full Codebase Audit
*Reviewed: 2026-06-10 | Repos: designflow-backend, designflow-bff, designflow-data-syncing, designflow-frontend, designflow-item-master, designflow-tracking*

---

## How to read this file

Each finding has:
- **Repo** — which of the 6 repos it lives in
- **File + line** — exactly where to find it
- **Severity** — 🔴 High (security or real bug) / 🟠 Medium (likely problem or major inefficiency) / 🟡 Low (cleanup, dead code, style)
- **Confidence** — Certain / Likely / Possible
- **What it is** — plain description
- **What it causes** — real-world consequence

Findings are grouped first by severity, then by repo.

---

## Session Remediation Status — 2026-06-10

What changed:
The `sandbox-albert` working copies now include fixes for `BACKEND-H1`, `BACKEND-H3`, `ITEM-MASTER-H2`, `TRACKING-H2`, `FRONTEND-H2`, and `FRONTEND-H3`. The backend login token path no longer has a master-password bypass; backend AI proxy routes, item-master full-catalog route, and tracking lead-time routes now require `authRole(...)`. Frontend role-marked routes now use `RoleGuard`, and the user-management route is admin-only.

Why:
These were unauthenticated or bypassable access paths with security and billing impact.

Future sessions should:
Treat those findings as remediated in local code only after checking the relevant repo status. Verify with `designflow-backend/tests/unit/login-token.helper.test.js`, `designflow-backend/tests/unit/openrouter.router.test.js`, `designflow-item-master/tests/unit/item-library.router.test.js`, `designflow-tracking/tests/unit/lead-time.router.test.js`, `designflow-frontend/src/app/app-routing.module.spec.ts`, and `designflow-frontend/src/app/pages/auth/helpers/role-guard.service.spec.ts`.

---

## Table of Contents

1. [🔴 High Severity — Security](#-high-severity--security)

*** Begin Patch
*** Update File: C:\repos\dflow plm\designflow-backend\docs\architecture.md
@@
 ### RFQ / product grid
 - `POST /api/grid/getAllItem`, `getGridHeader`, `saveGridLayout`, `getCustomGridLayout`
 - Vendor quotes (in `routes/lib.router.js`): `POST /updateVendor`, `/sendPriceReq`, `/addingVendor`. **Re-quote (stale price refresh):** `POST /requestRequote` (internal roles only — flags a factory's quote and emails the factory for a fresh price) and `POST /keepPrice` (vendor reconfirms the current price, clearing the flag). Both resolve the quote by `quote_id` (`RFQVendor_id`) and run through the `vendorMayModifyQuote` ownership guard. See `models/lib.model.js` `Customer.requestRequote` / `Customer.keepPrice`.
 - **Archive:** `POST /archiveRFQItem` archives/un-archives an item. For internal roles it toggles the global `RFQItem.rfqItem_archive`; for vendors it toggles a **per-vendor** `RFQVendor_archived` on their own quote row (so it never hides the item from Sourcing or other factories). On a vendor's list load, `Customer.getAll` auto-archives that factory's priced quotes older than 3 months (`helpers/archive-stale-quotes.js`). A vendor un-archive is sticky (`RFQVendor_archive_optout`); `requestRequote`, a fresh price, and `keepPrice` auto-un-archive / reset the cycle. See AGENTS.md §11.
+
+### Selected Vendor Persistence
+
+What changed:
+`POST /selectedVendor` maps the RFQ grid payload through `utility.mapRFQItem()` and updates the matching `RFQItem` row. It looks up by `rfqItem_id` when present and falls back to `rfqItem_style_number` for older callers.
+
+Why:
+The selected factory is stored on `RFQItem.rfqItem_choosen_vendor`; writing this payload to `GridLayout` targets the wrong table and does not persist the item choice.
+
+Future sessions should:
+Keep selected-vendor changes on `RFQItem`, not `GridLayout`, and run `tests/unit/selected-vendor.model.test.js` when changing `/selectedVendor` or RFQ chosen-vendor mapping.
 
 ### Other
 - `/api/customers/**`, `/api/item-size/**`, `/api/item_depth/**`, `/api/comment/**`, `/api/role/**`, `/api/autofill/**`, `/std/**`
*** End Patch

payload.input:
*** Begin Patch
*** Update File: C:\repos\dflow plm\designflow-item-master\docs\system-architecture.md
@@
 The item-master service owns the active API boundary for these screens. It reads
 and writes item master tables, while data-sync can populate or refresh item data
 from Coldlion into the same schema family.
+
+### Bounded Item Number Generation
+
+What changed:
+`ItemLibrary.createItem()` generates `LIB-...` item numbers with at most 25 collision attempts. Each attempt checks both `itemHeader.item_num_id` and `RFQItem.rfqItem_style_number` once, then returns a 409-style response if no unique value is found.
+
+Why:
+The previous collision checks looped inside each helper until a queried value disappeared, so an existing item number could hang the request and hammer the database indefinitely.
+
+Future sessions should:
+Do not reintroduce unbounded `while` loops in item-number generation. Keep retry limits explicit and run `tests/unit/item-number-generation.test.js` when changing `services/item_library.service.js` item creation.
 
 ### Production and Licensing Tracking
 
 Production and licensing tracking route through `/api/tracking`.
*** End Patch

-and writes item master tables, while data-sync can populate or refresh item data
-from Coldlion into the same schema family.

-### Production and Licensing Tracking

-Production and licensing tracking route through `/api/tracking`.
+The item-master service owns the active API boundary for these screens. It reads
+and writes item master tables, while data-sync can populate or refresh item data
+from Coldlion into the same schema family.
+
+### Bounded Item Number Generation
+
+What changed:
+`ItemLibrary.createItem()` generates `LIB-...` item numbers with at most 25 collision attempts. Each attempt checks both `itemHeader.item_num_id` and `RFQItem.rfqItem_style_number` once, then returns a 409-style response if no unique value is found.
+
+Why:
+The previous collision checks looped inside each helper until a queried value disappeared, so an existing item number could hang the request and hammer the database indefinitely.
+
+Future sessions should:
+Do not reintroduce unbounded `while` loops in item-number generation. Keep retry limits explicit and run `tests/unit/item-number-generation.test.js` when changing `services/item_library.service.js` item creation.
+
+### Production and Licensing Tracking
+
+Production and licensing tracking route through `/api/tracking`.

For internal roles it toggles the global `RFQItem.rfqItem_archive`; for vendors it toggles a **per-vendor** `RFQVendor_archived` on their own quote row (so it never hides the item from Sourcing or other factories). C:\repos\dflow plm\designflow-backend\docs\architecture.md:139:### Selected Vendor Persistence
C:\repos\dflow plm\designflow-backend\docs\architecture.md:142:`POST /selectedVendor` maps the RFQ grid payload through `utility.mapRFQItem()` and updates the matching `RFQItem` row. It looks up by `rfqItem_id` when present and falls back to `rfqItem_style_number` for older callers.
C:\repos\dflow plm\designflow-backend\docs\architecture.md:145:The selected factory is stored on `RFQItem.rfqItem_choosen_vendor`; writing this payload to `GridLayout` targets the wrong table and does not persist the item choice.
C:\repos\dflow plm\designflow-backend\docs\architecture.md:148:Keep selected-vendor changes on `RFQItem`, not `GridLayout`, and run `tests/unit/selected-vendor.model.test.js` when changing `/selectedVendor` or RFQ chosen-vendor mapping.
C:\repos\dflow plm\designflow-backend\docs\architecture.md:276:**Quote mixed-case identifiers.** Sequelize quotes all identifiers, so a model column is queried case-sensitively (e.g. `"rfqItem_price_sales_snapshots"`). An unquoted `ADD COLUMN IF NOT EXISTS rfqItem_foo` is folded to lowercase `rfqitem_foo` by Postgres and never matches Sequelize's quoted `SELECT` — every `RFQItem.findAll` then throws and the RFQ grid returns SYS-002. This was a live incident; see AGENTS.md §14.
C:\repos\dflow plm\designflow-backend\docs\architecture.md:278:**`RFQItem.rfqItem_price_sales_snapshots`** (`TEXT`, nullable): per-row JSON map of `price_sales` colId → `{ duty_rate, duty_dollar, container_price, at }`, snapshotting the cost inputs at the moment each "Price for Sales" cell was last populated (surfaced as a cell tooltip). Mapped both directions in `helpers/utility.js` (`remapRFQItem` / `mapRFQItem`).
C:\repos\dflow plm\designflow-backend\docs\architecture.md:280:**`RFQVendor.requote_requested`** (`BOOLEAN DEFAULT false`, added 2026-06): true when Sourcing has asked a factory to refresh a stale quote. Set by `requestRequote`; cleared by `keepPrice`, by a new vendor price in `updateVendorData`. Surfaced to the frontend in `helpers/utility.js` `remapRFQItem` (drives the orange price cell + bilingual "Quoted <date>" tooltip in the RFQ detail grid). The migration quotes the identifier (`"requote_requested"`) per the SYS-002 rule even though it is all-lowercase.
C:\repos\dflow plm\designflow-backend\docs\architecture.md:282:**`RFQVendor.RFQVendor_archived`** (`BOOLEAN DEFAULT false`, added 2026-06): per-vendor archive flag on a factory's own quote row. Drives the vendor's archive/active tab split in `Customer.getAll` (vendors filter `$RFQVendors.RFQVendor_archived$`; internal roles still use the global `RFQItem.rfqItem_archive`). Auto-set by the lazy sweep `helpers/archive-stale-quotes.js` `archiveStaleVendorQuotes()` for priced quotes whose `quote_date` is >3 months old. ## Summary
- fixes `/selectedVendor` so the selected factory persists on `RFQItem` instead of writing to `GridLayout`
- keeps the existing RFQ item lookup behavior: prefer `rfqItem_id`, fall back to `rfqItem_style_number`
- includes the sandbox auth hardening already on this branch for the paid AI proxy/login bypass
- updates backend architecture docs for selected-vendor persistence

## Validation
- `npm test -- --runTestsByPath tests/unit/selected-vendor.model.test.js`
- `npm test -- --runInBand`

- `npm test -- --runInBand`
